  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Which scope do you want for “make this stronger”? A) Prose-only tightening: sentence/paragraph edits for clarity, concision, and a more assertive tone without changing structure or claims (primarily R1.6); or B) Argument-level strengthening: clarify and foreground the contribution vs prior work, align claims with reported evidence, and reorganize the section as needed while also polishing prose, without adding new experiments (R1.1–R1.5 plus R1.6).",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Strengthening may require reframing the thesis and contribution to foreground the joint clarification-and-retrieval objective and its motivation.",
    "R1.2": "A stronger section benefits from a clear argument chain linking ambiguity in IR to the model design and then to the empirical validation.",
    "R1.3": "Claims like a 14 F1 gain and being first end-to-end must be tightly aligned with the reported benchmarks and slices described.",
    "R1.4": "To make the contribution feel stronger, the delta versus nearest prior work and the 'first' claim need careful positioning.",
    "R1.5": "Improved impact may come from reordering to reduce repetition and to lead with the most compelling results and contributions.",
    "R1.6": "Crisper, more assertive wording and removal of redundancy directly increase perceived strength at the sentence level.",
    "RX.1": "Given the brief request, we must flag uncertainty about whether the intent is prose-only polish or deeper argument restructuring.",
    "RX.2": "Any effort to sound stronger must avoid overstating novelty or performance beyond what the text supports.",
    "RX.3": "The work should stay within the provided passage and not introduce new data, results, or external claims.",
    "RX.4": "Overly forceful language can mislead readers about capabilities or generality, so caution is needed to prevent downstream confusion.",
    "RX.5": "Edits should preserve clear traceability between strengthened claims and the specific sentences, datasets, and metrics cited in the excerpt."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Which scope do you want for “make this stronger”? A) Prose-only tightening: sentence/paragraph edits for clarity, concision, and a more assertive tone without changing structure or claims (primarily R1.6); or B) Argument-level strengthening: clarify and foreground the contribution vs prior work, align claims with reported evidence, and reorganize the section as needed while also polishing prose, without adding new experiments (R1.1–R1.5 plus R1.6).

rationale.R1.1: Strengthening may require reframing the thesis and contribution to foreground the joint clarification-and-retrieval objective and its motivation.
rationale.R1.2: A stronger section benefits from a clear argument chain linking ambiguity in IR to the model design and then to the empirical validation.
rationale.R1.3: Claims like a 14 F1 gain and being first end-to-end must be tightly aligned with the reported benchmarks and slices described.
rationale.R1.4: To make the contribution feel stronger, the delta versus nearest prior work and the 'first' claim need careful positioning.
rationale.R1.5: Improved impact may come from reordering to reduce repetition and to lead with the most compelling results and contributions.
rationale.R1.6: Crisper, more assertive wording and removal of redundancy directly increase perceived strength at the sentence level.
rationale.RX.1: Given the brief request, we must flag uncertainty about whether the intent is prose-only polish or deeper argument restructuring.
rationale.RX.2: Any effort to sound stronger must avoid overstating novelty or performance beyond what the text supports.
rationale.RX.3: The work should stay within the provided passage and not introduce new data, results, or external claims.
rationale.RX.4: Overly forceful language can mislead readers about capabilities or generality, so caution is needed to prevent downstream confusion.
rationale.RX.5: Edits should preserve clear traceability between strengthened claims and the specific sentences, datasets, and metrics cited in the excerpt.
